summary: This brief post contrasts Apple's secretive product development approach with Microsoft's tendency to talk about products years before they ship. Joel poses the question of whether companies should build buzz by discussing products under development or maintain secrecy until launch. The post serves as a teaser linking to his longer article 'Mouth Wide Shut' where he explores this question in depth.
key_points: Apple surprises people with product launches despite devoted watchers trying to uncover secrets
Microsoft discusses products years before shipping, with external testers using .NET long before release
The core strategic question is whether pre-launch buzz or launch-day surprise is the better approach
money_quotes: When Apple releases a new product, they tend to surprise the heck out of people, even the devoted Apple-watchers who have spent the last few months riffling through garbage dumpsters at One Infinite Loop.
Microsoft, on the other hand, can't stop talking about products that are mere glimmers in someone's eye.
Should you talk endlessly about your products under development, in hopes of building buzz, or should you hold off until you've got something ready to go?
key_insight: Companies face a fundamental strategic choice between building pre-launch buzz through openness and maintaining secrecy for maximum launch impact.
